name: Electromagnetic Reconnaissance
description: Detection and assessment of electromagnetic emissions in an area of interest in order to build the electromagnetic order of battle.

name: Direction Finding
description: Procedures used to determine the location of emitters. Techniques such as Lines of Bearing, Cuts, or Fixes may be employed.

name: Electromagnetic Probing
description: Intentional introduction of radiation into adversary systems in order to learn their specific functions and operational capabilities.

name: Directed-Energy Weapons
description: Weapons that emit highly concentrated energy to damage or destroy targets.

name: Lasers
description: Directed-energy weapon that uses optical beams to blind sensors or destroy components.

name: High-Power Microwave
description: Emission of concentrated beams of electromagnetic energy to disrupt or destroy electronic systems.

name: Electromagnetic Pulse
description: Strong bursts of radiation used to permanently damage electrical systems.

name: Electromagnetic Jamming
description: Use of deliberately radiated energy to reduce the enemy's effective use of the electromagnetic spectrum.

name: Spot Jamming
description: Interference concentrated on a single frequency.

name: Barrage Jamming
description: Simultaneous interference across multiple frequencies over a wide frequency band.

name: Sweep Jamming
description: Rapid sweeping of power across a range of frequencies.

name: Follower Jamming
description: Passive system that detects an enemy transmission and automatically attacks it.

name: Standoff Jamming
description: Interference conducted from a protected stationary position within friendly areas to support an advance.

name: Escort Jamming
description: Use of jamming platforms that physically accompany maneuver forces.

name: CREW
description: Systems that block radio-controlled explosive device detonators.

name: Electromagnetic Deception
description: Use of deliberately radiated energy to transmit misleading information.

name: Simulative Deception
description: Creation of false emission profiles to simulate capabilities that do not exist.

name: Manipulative Deception
description: Use of real communication signals to transmit misleading indicators of friendly intentions.

name: Imitative Deception
description: Imitation of enemy emissions, such as entering their networks using their call signs, to cause confusion.

name: Electromagnetic Intrusion
description: Intentional insertion of energy into enemy transmission paths to deceive operators or generate confusion.

name: Meaconing
description: Reception of navigation signals and retransmission on the same frequency to confuse the enemy.

name: Defensive EA
description: Reactive technique that uses electromagnetic energy to protect specific friendly platforms from incoming hostile threats.

name: Decoys
description: Self-propelled, towed, or stationary systems used to divert attacks.

name: Flares or Chaff
description: Objects used to confuse enemy missiles and radars.

name: Threat Warning
description: Use of sensors to detect and alert about immediate risks so countermeasures can be activated.

name: Electromagnetic Hardening
description: Filtering, shielding, and grounding actions used to protect systems against electromagnetic pulse or directed-energy weapons.

name: Electromagnetic Masking
description: Controlled radiation on friendly frequencies to conceal real emissions.

name: Terrain Masking
description: Tactical positioning of antennas behind terrain features or buildings to avoid enemy detection.

name: EMCON
description: Selective and controlled use of emitters to optimize command and control while minimizing detection by enemy sensors.

name: WARM
description: Keeping operational sensor features secret until combat to prevent the enemy from developing countermeasures.

name: Spread Spectrum
description: Deliberate variation of frequency to reduce susceptibility to interference.

name: Frequency Hopping
description: Rapid frequency changes to make interception and enemy jamming more difficult.

name: Minimizing Transmissions
description: Reduction of the number, duration, frequency, and predictability of electromagnetic transmissions in order to decrease the probability of detection.

name: Minimum Transmit Power Modes
description: Use of the lowest possible transmission power level required to maintain the communication link or sensor function.

name: Electromagnetic Warfare Reprogramming
description: Deliberate alteration of software, hardware, or tactics of systems in response to changes in the enemy or the environment.
